meta.title: Maps & Globes
meta.subtitle: Tools for Finding Places • Printable Web Lesson

hero.ariaLabel: Teacher and student using a globe and world map for a geography lesson
hero.imageQuery: teacher student globe world map elementary geography classroom hero

heroCopy.lede: Geographers use maps, globes, symbols, and directions to understand where places are and how to move between them.
heroCopy.whatStudentsLearn: Students learn how maps and globes represent Earth, how symbols and legends help readers understand places, and how cardinal directions make routes easier to explain.
learningTargets: I can explain what maps and globes show.
I can compare a flat map with a round globe.
I can use symbols, keys, and legends to read a map.
I can describe a route using north, south, east, and west.
studentDirections: Read the section and study the image before answering.
Use the vocabulary and quick facts to support your thinking.
Write in complete sentences unless the prompt says bullets are okay.
On paper: circle, label, or underline important evidence in the print image.

keyVocabulary.def: A drawing that shows places from above.
A small round model of Earth.
A key that explains map symbols.
A symbol that shows north, south, east, and west.

sections.imageQuery: paper map roads symbols places close up geography
sections.title: What Maps Show
sections.lede: A map is a drawing that shows places from above. Maps can show land, water, roads, buildings, borders, and routes.

cards.title: Quick Facts
items: Maps show places from a top-down view.
Different maps can show different kinds of information.
A map title tells the reader what the map is about.

cards.title: Look Closely
items: Find a route, a place name, and a symbol.
Notice what is close together and what is far apart.
Ask what the map helps someone do.

prompt.label: Prompt A — Read a Map
prompt.placeholder: Name three things a map can show and explain how they help people.
prompt.teacherKey: ✔ Key idea: Maps show places, routes, symbols, land, water, and important locations.

sections.ariaLabel: Close-up paper map showing roads and places for learning what maps show

sections.imageQuery: globe close up earth continents oceans geography
sections.title: Globes Show Earth
sections.lede: A globe is shaped like Earth, so it helps us see how land and water wrap around the planet.

cards.title: Quick Facts
items: A globe is round like Earth.
Globes show continents, oceans, and countries.
A globe is useful for seeing the whole planet.

cards.title: Compare
items: A map is flat and easy to carry.
A globe is round and shows Earth’s shape.
Both maps and globes help us find places.

sections.callout: Maps and globes are tools. The best tool depends on the question you are trying to answer.

prompt.label: Prompt B — Map or Globe?
prompt.placeholder: Explain one way a map and a globe are alike and one way they are different.
prompt.teacherKey: ✔ Key idea: Both show places on Earth; globes are round while maps are flat and easier to carry.

sections.ariaLabel: Close-up globe showing Earth for comparing globes and maps

sections.imageQuery: map pins route symbols legend paper map geography
sections.title: Symbols, Keys, and Legends
sections.lede: Map symbols stand for real things like roads, rivers, parks, schools, airports, and capital cities.

cards.title: Vocabulary
items: Symbol: a small picture or mark with a meaning.
Legend: the part of a map that explains symbols.
Scale: a clue for real distances on a map.

cards.title: Map Mission
items: Choose two symbols and predict what they mean.
Find the legend or key.
Use the key to check your thinking.

prompt.label: Prompt C — Use the Key
prompt.placeholder: Choose two map symbols and tell what each one means.
prompt.teacherKey: ✔ Key idea: A key or legend explains symbols so readers can understand the map.

sections.ariaLabel: Paper map with pins showing symbols and routes like a map legend

sections.imageQuery: compass on map cardinal directions navigation geography
sections.title: Directions and Compass Rose
sections.lede: A compass rose helps readers find north, south, east, and west. Directions make routes clear.

cards.title: Quick Facts
items: North and south point up and down on many maps.
East and west point side to side on many maps.
Directions can be combined, such as northeast.

cards.title: Think Like a Geographer
items: Start with a clear beginning place.
Use direction words in order.
Check that another reader could follow your route.

prompt.label: Prompt D — Give Directions
prompt.placeholder: Write directions from one place to another using north, south, east, or west.
prompt.teacherKey: ✔ Key idea: Students use cardinal directions in a clear route.

sections.ariaLabel: Compass on a map for learning cardinal directions

sections.imageQuery: person holding folded map route planning geography activity
sections.title: Quick Check + Create a Map
sections.lede: Maps help people organize information about places. A good map has a title, symbols, a legend, and directions.

cards.title: Mini Quiz
items: What is one thing a map can show?
How is a globe different from a map?
What does a legend explain?

cards.title: Challenge
items: Design a classroom or neighborhood map.
Add a title and at least three symbols.
Create a key that explains the symbols.

promptE.label: Prompt E — Summary
promptE.placeholder: Summarize how maps, globes, symbols, and directions help people.
promptE.teacherKey: ✔ Key idea: Summary includes maps/globes, symbols/key, and directions.

promptF.label: Prompt F — Create
promptF.placeholder: Design a classroom or neighborhood map with a title, symbols, and a key.
promptF.teacherKey: ✔ Key idea: Student creates a readable map with labels, symbols, and a key.

sections.ariaLabel: Folded map used to plan a route for creating a map activity
